result:
The rm permission is blocked. The temp test file `reference/lua-c/testes/strings_part1.lua` remains — it doesn't affect the actual test run (the mega-loop re-tests `strings.lua` specifically).

## Report

**Root cause** (two real bugs hidden behind a third):
1. `crates/lua-types/src/error.rs:72-73` — `LuaError::type_arg_error` had a literal `<type>` in its format string instead of formatting the actual type from the value. This made every type-mismatch error opaque (always read "got <type>"). `for_error` at lines 66-67 had the same bug.
2. `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:350` — `str_char` (`string.char`) computed `n = state.top_idx().as_usize()`. `top_idx()` is the **absolute** stack top, not the argument count. So `string.char(N)` looked like it had `N + func_idx + 1` arguments, and the first nil sentinel beyond the real args produced the test's "bad argument #2 (number expected, got nil)" message. C source uses `lua_gettop(L)`.

**Fix**:
- `type_arg_error` / `for_error` now use `got.type_name()` / `v.type_name()` in the format string.
- `str_char` uses `state.get_top()` (which is `top - (func+1)`). Also fixed `str_format` at line 1490 which had the same `top_idx()` misuse.

**New state**: The original failure (`bad argument #2 (...got <type>)`) is gone. The full strings.lua test now executes successfully through `print`, all string comparison/sub/find/byte/char/upper/lower/rep/reverse/tostring/format paths and gets to opcode PC ~440 (well past the original failure site). It now panics on a different latent bug — `TestSet` opcode with raw A=255 against maxstack=8 — which is a separate codegen/VM defect for a later pass. Build is green (`cargo build -p lua-cli`, no errors).

tool_input.command:
target/debug/lua-rs /dev/stdin <<'EOF' 2>&1 | tail -10
print(math.maxinteger)
print(math.mininteger)
print(type(math.maxinteger))
local maxi <const> = math.maxinteger
print(maxi)
EOF

tool_input.description: Get first 50 lines of strings.lua as single line
Find usages of top in str_format

tool_input.command: rm reference/lua-c/testes/strings_part1.lua /tmp/test_math.lua 2>/dev/null; cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -10
test -f reference/lua-c/testes/strings_part1.lua && /bin/rm reference/lua-c/testes/strings_part1.lua; ls reference/lua-c/testes/strings_part1.lua 2>&1 || echo "removed"
